token_consumption.cost_variance: 150% token difference between cheapest and most expensive

key_findings: 37.5% of commands VIOLATE the sacred complexity limit - that's not engineering, that's chaos
Mean complexity of 4.00/5.00 means you're living dangerously close to the edge
240% execution time variance suggests some commands are optimized like a drunk sloth
Token consumption varies by 150% - somebody clearly never heard of efficiency

adaptive-complexity-router.md.roast: This thing routes complexity by BEING complex. Meta-irony at its finest. It's like hiring a pyromaniac as a fire chief.

intelligent-refactor-session.md.roast: 22 thinking blocks and 534 lines? This isn't refactoring, it's philosophical meditation with code on the side.

ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md.roast: Takes 85 seconds and 20k tokens to 'think'. My calculator from 1985 had more efficiency per byte.

adhd-context-switch.md.roast: Actually follows the rules. In this codebase, that counts as a miracle. Someone deserves a cookie.

safe-code-beautifier.md.roast: Makes code beautiful through 427 lines of complexity. Like using a bulldozer for eyebrow threading.

immediate_actions: REFACTOR the 3 complexity violators immediately - they're technical debt disasters waiting to happen
Implement complexity caps in CI/CD - if it hits 5, it gets rejected like a bad dating profile
Standardize execution times - 240% variance is unprofessional chaos
architectural_improvements: Split monolithic commands into focused micro-commands
Create shared utility libraries for common thinking patterns
Implement command composition instead of mega-commands
performance_optimizations: Token budgets for each command category
Execution time SLAs with automated monitoring

complexity_score.interpretation: We can be 95% confident the true mean complexity is dangerously high

risk_assessment.overall_risk_level: HIGH - These commands are technical debt time bombs
